data.title: Maanshan Iron & Steel Updates on Capital Reduction Agreement
data.description:
Maanshan Iron & Steel Co ( (HK:0323) ) has shared an announcement.

Maanshan Iron & Steel Company Limited announced a supplemental update regarding its Capital Reduction Agreement with Baowu Water. The agreement involves a reduction in the company’s shareholding in Baowu Water to 3.151%, resulting in a change in accounting treatment to financial assets measured at fair value through other comprehensive income. This transaction does not involve cash and will not materially impact the company’s cash flow, but it signifies a shift in the company’s influence over Baowu Water.

data.title: A Metaverse Company Faces Winding-Up Petition Amid Financial Challenges
data.description:
The latest announcement is out from A Metaverse Company ( (HK:1616) ).

A Metaverse Company is facing a winding-up petition filed by BEITAI INVESTMENT LP due to an outstanding debt of approximately HK$33.4 million from a subscription agreement for unsecured bonds. The petition, scheduled for a court hearing on 26 November 2025, could lead to significant operational and financial implications, including restrictions on share transfers and potential suspension of services by the Hong Kong Securities Clearing Company Limited. The appointed provisional liquidators are currently focused on securing the company’s assets and exploring options regarding the petition.

data.title: Hevol Services Group Announces Board Restructuring
data.description:
Hevol Services Group Co. Limited ( (HK:6093) ) has provided an update.

Hevol Services Group Co. Limited has announced changes in its board composition, reflecting a strategic shift in its governance structure. Dr. Li Yongrui has resigned from his roles as an independent non-executive director and chairman of both the Nomination and Remuneration Committees to focus on other business commitments. In his place, Dr. Chen Lei will serve as the chairman of the Remuneration Committee, Mr. Qian Hongji will take over as chairman of the Nomination Committee, and Ms. Hu Hongfang will join the Remuneration Committee as a member. These changes are effective from September 26, 2025, and are expected to bring fresh perspectives to the company’s board operations.

data.title: Shanghai Henlius Initiates Global Trial for Cancer Treatment Biosimilar
data.description:
Shanghai Henlius Biotech, Inc. Class H ( (HK:2696) ) just unveiled an update.

Shanghai Henlius Biotech, Inc. has initiated an international multi-center phase 1 clinical trial for HLX17, a biosimilar of pembrolizumab, in patients with multiple resected solid tumors in mainland China. This trial aims to evaluate the pharmacokinetic profile, efficacy, safety, and immunogenicity of HLX17 compared to KEYTRUDA, with plans to expand the trial to the United States, Europe, and Australia. The announcement marks a significant step in the company’s efforts to position itself in the global oncology market, leveraging the high global sales of pembrolizumab, which reached approximately USD 32.056 billion in 2024.
